title: How many languages does Asuka speak
display_title: How many languages does Asuka speak
wikitext:
[]: How many languages does Asuka speak?             Quote Post by Duke » Tue Mar 10, 2009 10:10 pm I know she speaks German, and Japanese, but since her actual nationality is American, do you think she can speak English too?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Mar 10, 2009 10:28 pm It's a possibility, but do keep in mind she was raised completely in Germany with the intent to ship her off to Japan ASAP. English might not have gotten covered because of the difficulty of learning Japanese (she was still having trouble with reading and writing, after all).

[]: Re: How many languages does Asuka speak?             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 10, 2009 11:40 pm Duke wrote: but since her actual nationality is American

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Wed Mar 11, 2009 12:35 am Reichu, her Nationality is American because Langley was an American. She's like 1/4th German, 1/4th Japanese, and like 1/2 American.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Mar 11, 2009 12:42 am Duke wrote: Reichu, her Nationality is American because Langley was an American. She's like 1/4th German, 1/4th Japanese, and like 1/2 American.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Mar 11, 2009 12:57 am Uh, we seem to be having some confusion here, so lemme cover this: ETHNICALLY, Asuka is 1/4 German, 1/4 Japanese, and 1/2 something else, probably a white European group, as she's too light to be anything else. People cannot be American ethnically; the closest is NATIVE American. As far as NATIONALITY goes, Asuka is presumably a joint German/Japanese citizen, as there is no evidence that she has ever lived in America. People CAN have an American nationality. 300 million people today do. As far as the original question goes: Asuka probably knows no more English than the average German.

[]:             Quote Post by bryan2369 » Wed Mar 11, 2009 12:57 am Of course she'd learn English. German schools teach English at a young age, and since Asuka went to a University, she'd probably learn more for the credits, plus they have foreign professors in the U's. Also, I think Asuka is 1/4 Japanese, 2/4 German and 1/4 American. Her father is 1/2 German and 1/2 American, her Mother is 1/2 German and 1/2 Japanese. Her father's background is merely hinted on, but it makes sense. Obviously her mother can't be American hence her name, so it must fall on her father. My $.02

[]:             Quote Post by bryan2369 » Wed Mar 11, 2009 1:01 am Um, I'm a native American as I was born in America. Not American Indian, of course, but I am a native (small n) American, my passport says so.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Wed Mar 11, 2009 1:06 am Why the fuck can't we count American as an ethnicity? They count Japanese as an ethnicity, even though most of Japan was populated by the CHINESE. So, in my eyes, you CAN be ethnically American, because even though it is a large melting pot, WE MELT TOGETHER AND MAKE OUR OWN ETHNICITY.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Wed Mar 11, 2009 1:40 am I was about to say something along those same lines. Sure, our own roots may not extend as far back as say, the Chinese, but I would say that the large number of different races and ethnic groups interbreeding and mixing their genetic traits will create a new ethnicity. I'm sure if we go far enough back in history we'll find that many of the world's "pure" races were actually created by mixing gene pools. Apologies in advance for this tangent.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Mar 11, 2009 2:31 am Girl can't speak German. You listen to her in Episode 22, most distractingly hilarious moment in Eva history.* *Going by the original Japanese audio of course. Though in the German dub it's quite funny too.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Mar 11, 2009 2:50 am Isn't it stated somewhere in the official small print that she is a US citizen? I would expect her to (Miyamura's difficulty with the language aside -- not every seiyuu can be as awesome as Kakihara Tetsuya) have native German, fluent English and conversational Japanese -- just that she won't have spent the hundreds of hours memorizing the faux-Chinese chicken-scratches used in the written form. And probably a smattering of some other European languages on the side.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 11, 2009 3:27 am There is no such thing as a Native American in any sense other than just "was born here"... anyone claiming otherwise is really just descended from an earlier wave of immigrants.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Mar 11, 2009 3:30 am The same could be said of anywhere outside of Kenya.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Mar 11, 2009 6:00 am Japanese Asuka: Japanese and German and maybe some english, English Asuka: English and German Spanish Asuka: Spanish and German German Asuka: German and German

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 11, 2009 10:12 am bryan2369 wrote: Her father is 1/2 German and 1/2 American

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Wed Mar 11, 2009 2:39 pm Legendary wrote: As far as the original question goes: Asuka probably knows no more English than the average German.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Wed Mar 11, 2009 3:51 pm Yeah, rofl. All the "average" germans I've met were VERY fluent in English o_O I mean, I can play in a German server in several games, with NATIVE Germans, and they pretty much ALL speak t3h English, and their grammar isn't bad either.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Thu Mar 12, 2009 9:29 pm Asuka is clearly stated in the series as being a US Citizen. You can be born outside of USA and still get a US passport, of course, especially if one or both of your parents are already citizens. It's logical to assume that Mr. Langley is from the US and just registered his daughter's citizenship when she was born. As far as the "return to Japan" thing, it's not really clear if she spent her whole childhood in Germany, or if she was even born there (is there sauce for that?). She could have been in Japan in her very younger years, especially if she had family from there. Certainly, though, she was in Germany long enough for her to identify with being German. I don't think she would think of herself in any other way. For languages, graduates of the German school system have to get proficiency in at least one other language other than German, so English is a good bet. She'd pick up another language in university as well (Japanese, perhaps?).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 12, 2009 10:00 pm Grand Duke of Yashima wrote: Asuka is clearly stated in the series as being a US Citizen.
